title: Dedupe events, keeping the first occurrence
prompt_md:
Retries and at-least-once delivery mean your backend often receives the **same event more than once**. A common fix is to drop duplicates by id, keeping the **first** time you saw each one (order preserved).

Write `dedupe_by_id(items)` where `items` is a list of dicts each containing an `"id"`. Return a new list with only the **first** occurrence of each `id`, in original order.
hints: Track which ids you've already kept in a set.
Append an item only if its id hasn't been seen yet.
